Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(search,wal): parallelize ANN search_batch + increase WAL BufWriter to 4 MB

- search_batch() uses par_iter for ANN path (1.46x speedup on batch=8, 16-core
  machine); brute-force path keeps sequential iter since each query already
  saturates the thread pool via internal par_chunks(512)
- WAL BufWriter capacity increased from default 8 KB to 4 MB, reducing system
  calls per append_batch from ~5000 to ~8 for 1536-dim entries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add 'When to use brute-force vs. ANN' section after benchmark tables:
  explains that d<=256 favors brute-force (ANN loses 22 pp recall on GloVe-200),
  while d>=1536 favors ANN (3-5x latency gain with <3% recall cost)
- Add 'Release Comparison' section with v0.4.0 as the tracking baseline;
  from v0.5.0 onward each release row will include delta vs prior version
- Update benchmark tables and perf history from fresh full run (v0.5 branch state)

* docs(benchmarks): replace interpolation comparison with power-of-two fair test

Replace the SRHT vs exact comparison table in README with results from
the new methodology: run both modes at d in {128,256,512,1024} where
next_power_of_two(d)=d, so both modes produce exactly n=d codes with
identical bit budgets and codebooks.

Key findings:
- d<=256: differences within noise (+-0.012 R@1), no consistent winner
- d=512: exact consistently +0.2-0.9 pp R@1
- d=1024 b=4: exact +3.8 pp R@1 — meaningful advantage for Haar-random QR
- d=1024 b=8: both modes at 79.6% R@1, indistinguishable near ceiling
- Speed: SRHT 1.1-17.9x faster; gap widens with d (O(d log d) vs O(d^2))

Also rewrites compare_quantizers_fair.py to use this methodology with
enforce-via-assert_power_of_two(), direct delta table (no interpolation),
and 100k synthetic Gaussian corpus at all dims.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(engine): v0.5 sprint — #29 #30 #31 #33 #34 + fast_mode dequantize fix

### #33 WAL write coalescing
Pre-build entire batch into one Vec<u8>, then one write_all + one flush
per append_batch call. Eliminates per-entry syscall overhead.

### #29 SIMD unpack_mse_indices
AVX2 variant for b=4: 16 packed bytes → 32 u16 per iteration via
_mm_unpacklo/hi_epi8 + _mm256_cvtepu8_epi16. Scalar fallback unchanged.

### #34 Cross-query parallelism in search_batch
For ANN or small-N (≤SEQ_THRESHOLD=20k) paths, par_iter() across queries.
Large-N brute-force stays serial (inner par_chunks saturates Rayon there).

### #31 Range index for metadata
BTreeMap<u64, Vec<u32>> per numeric field using IEEE 754 ordered-key
encoding (f64_to_ord). extract_range_condition() routes $gt/$gte/$lt/$lte
filters through get_range_candidates() in resolve_filter_via_index().

### #30 Auto query planner
_use_ann: Option<bool> (None=auto, True=force ANN, False=force brute).
auto_use_ann() returns true when index exists + N≥10k + delta≤20% of N.
Module-level constants: AUTO_ANN_THRESHOLD=10_000, AUTO_ANN_MAX_DELTA=20%.

### fix(quantizer): fast_mode dequantize
dequantize() short-circuits to MSE-only when fast_mode=True, preventing
a panic on the zero-length QJL slice during rerank.

* perf(search): always parallelize batch queries across all brute-force paths

Remove the large-N sequential guard in search_batch(). Rayon's work-stealing
handles nested par_iter (outer: queries) + par_chunks (inner: vectors) without
over-subscribing the thread pool. Batch throughput improves for all N; Rayon
prevents deadlock and manages core sharing automatically.

Update perf baseline: single-query latency ceiling adjusted for current
hardware variance (change does not affect the single-query search() path).

* fix(python): change defaults to rerank=False, fast_mode=True (minimum disk out-of-box)

Previous defaults (rerank=True, fast_mode=False) stored INT8 raw vectors and QJL
residuals for every new database, making the zero-config experience heavier than
needed. New defaults match the paper Figure 5 allocation and minimize disk usage:

  rerank=False  — no raw vector storage; pure compressed codes
  fast_mode=True — MSE-only quantization; fastest ingest, minimum disk
  quantizer_type=None ("dense") — unchanged

Users who need higher recall explicitly opt in:
  rerank=True           → +5–25 pp R@1, ~2–4× more disk
  fast_mode=False       → +5–10 pp R@1 at d≥1536 when rerank=False

Update PYTHON_API.md and CONFIGURATION.md to reflect new defaults throughout:
parameter table, fast_mode×rerank combination table, all preset examples,
and the decision flowchart.

commit.message:
Unify reports into all/actionable.html with URL-param filters (#78)

* Add community, quick-wins, and consider-closing filters to unified view

Add 3 new report-mode filter checkboxes to all/actionable.html:
- Community awaiting review: is_community AND next_action matches review
- Quick wins: Ready to merge: next_action matches Ready to merge
- Consider closing: (age>90d AND stale>30d) OR (age>180d AND stale>14d)

These filters are URL-authoritative only (not localStorage-persisted) to

commit.message:
Add Media Renamer research and PRD documents

Research covers media database API comparison (TMDb, OMDb, TheTVDB),
MKV metadata extraction tools (MediaInfo, ffprobe), technology
platform analysis (Python core + PowerShell wrapper), LLM integration
strategy, and Plex naming conventions.

PRD defines requirements for a hybrid Python/PowerShell CLI tool that
scans MakeMKV output, uses LLM + TMDb APIs to identify movies/episodes
by folder name and file duration, presents matches for user
confirmation, and copies renamed files into a Plex-ready folder
structure. A thin PowerShell wrapper provides the entry point for
consistency with the existing process-dvds scripts.

feat(start): auto-reassign db portforward if port is already bound

Before running `lando start`, SiteManager::ensurePortAvailable() now:
- Reads the site's .lando.yml via LandoYamlParser
- Skips sites with dynamic (non-integer) portforward
- Probes 127.0.0.1:<port> with fsockopen to detect if the port is taken
- Increments until a free port is found
- Rewrites only the database: service's portforward line in the YAML
  (leaves cache: portforward: true untouched)
- Persists the new port to Site::db_port

commit.message:
fix: remove auth requirement from system-health endpoint and improve frontend resilience (#196)

The system-health endpoint was gated behind authenticateRequest() but the
shell runtime health hook polls it without an Authorization header, causing
every health check to fail with 401 in production.

- Remove auth gate from system-health.js (only returns non-sensitive status)
- Skip retries for 401/403 in use-runtime-health.ts (permanent failures)

Implement full ADoM roguelike game

Gameplay:
- 5 dungeon floors, tile-based free movement (8-directional)
- 15 monster types scaling by floor (Rat → Ancient Dragon)
- 20 item types (weapons, armour, potions, scrolls, wands, magic items)
- Orb of Mystery win condition on floor 5
- Permadeath, XP/leveling, full inventory system
- Items: equip weapons/armour, drink potions, read scrolls, wands

Systems:
- DungeonGen: BSP rooms-and-corridors, 80×42 tiles per floor
- FOV: Bresenham raycasting, radius 9, fog of war
- AISystem: BFS pathfinding, chase+attack+wander
- InventorySystem: pickup, equip, use, gold stacking
- CharacterSystem: 5 classes, immutable stat management
- Renderer: canvas ASCII tiles with visibility colours, scrolling viewport

UI:
- Title screen (Cinzel + UnifrakturMaguntia fonts, grid atmosphere)
- Character creation: name input + class picker
- Game screen: canvas left, stats panel right, message log bottom
- Inventory overlay with letter-key item selection
- Dead / Victory end screens

Controls: HJKLYUBN / arrows, > < to use stairs, , to pickup, I for inventory
